titel: Temporary reduction of value-added tax on food products.
html:
The proposal suggests a temporary reduction of the value-added tax (VAT) rate on food from 12 percent to 6 percent. This proposal entails changes to the Value Added Tax Act (2023:200). The proposed amendment to the law is to come into force on April 1, 2026, and remain in effect until December 31, 2027.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:55

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Value Added Tax Act (2023:200).
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Value Added Tax Act (2023:200).

3. Prop. 2025/26:55 4.
tldr: The government wants to reduce the tax on food from 12 percent to 6 percent. This proposal aims to make grocery shopping more affordable for a limited time.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a change to the rules regarding value-added tax, often referred to as VAT. Currently, the tax on food is 12 percent, but the proposal would reduce it to 6 percent.

If the parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the lower tax rate will come into effect on April 1, 2026. This means that food could become cheaper for everyone who shops in stores.

This reduction is not permanent and is only intended to apply for a specific period. According to the plan, the new tax rate will be in effect until December 31, 2027.
